research_problem.value: The central challenge is how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale, enabling online process reward model (PRM) updates efficiently.
research_problem.evidence: We identify the central challenge as how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale, which enables online process reward model (PRM) update efficiently.

current_limitations.value: Current industry-leading models primarily depend on verifiable outcome rewards and have not yet demonstrated meaningful progress with dense rewards.
current_limitations.evidence: However, successful applications of dense rewards in RL for LLMs are limited, as current industry-leading models primarily depend on verifiable outcome rewards and have not yet demonstrated meaningful progress with dense rewards.

key_challenges.value: 1. Process rewards are hard to define. 2. PRM online updates are not scalable. 3. Explicit reward modeling brings extra cost.
key_challenges.evidence: C1. Process rewards are hard to define. C2. PRM online updates are not scalable. C3. Explicit reward modeling brings extra cost.

provenance.section_header: 2.2 KEY CHALLENGES IN SCALABLE DENSE REWARDS

main_idea.value: PRIME (Process Reinforcement through Implicit Rewards) enables online PRM updates using only policy rollouts and outcome labels through implicit process rewards.
main_idea.evidence: To address these challenges, we propose PRIME (Process Reinforcement through Implicit Rewards), which enables online PRM updates using only policy rollouts and outcome labels through implicit process rewards.

inspiration_source.value: The framework employs implicit process reward modeling to train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels.
inspiration_source.evidence: At its core, the framework employs recently proposed implicit process reward modeling to train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

objective.value: To train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels while enabling online learning of reward signals.
objective.evidence: This enables PRIME to perform online learning of reward signals using only outcome labels on policy rollouts.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

method.value: Implicit PRM enables training an ORM with outcome labels only while repurposing it as a PRM at inference.
method.evidence: In short, Implicit PRM enables training an ORM with outcome labels only while repurposing it as a PRM at inference.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

reward_calculation.value: r_φ(y_t) := β log(π_φ(y_t | y_<t) / π_ref(y_t | y_<t))
reward_calculation.evidence: The process rewards are obtained by: r_φ(y_t) := β log(π_φ(y_t | y_<t) / π_ref(y_t | y_<t)).

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

training_objective.value: The training objective is to update the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision.
training_objective.evidence: In PRIME, upon rollouts being generated and graded by the (ground truth) outcome verifier, we update the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

workflow.value: PRIME combines implicit process rewards with outcome rewards to calculate advantages and update the policy.
workflow.evidence: PRIME adopts implicit process rewards for efficient PRM online update, then integrates token-level dense rewards with outcome rewards in MC advantage estimate.

key_techniques.value: 1. Online updates of PRM. 2. Combining token-level and outcome rewards.
key_techniques.evidence: 1) To prevent overoptimization and reward hacking, it is crucial to update reward models online. 2) PRIME also serves as a general method to fuse token-level dense rewards and sparse outcome rewards.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

inference_enhancement.value: Using implicit process rewards allows for enhanced inference without the need for extensive annotation.
inference_enhancement.evidence: Unlike common PRMs that produce only step-level rewards, the Implicit PRM provides more fine-grained token-level rewards at no additional cost.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

experimental_setup.value: The experiments were conducted using Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base with PRIME after a lightweight SFT warmup stage.
experimental_setup.evidence: In experiments, we train Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base with PRIME after a lightweight SFT warmup stage.

algorithm_effectiveness.value: PRIME achieves a 15.1% average improvement across several key reasoning benchmarks over the SFT model.
algorithm_effectiveness.evidence: PRIME achieves a 15.1% average improvement across several key reasoning benchmarks over the SFT model.

acceleration_effect.value: PRIME achieves a 2.5x sample efficiency gain and a 6.9% performance improvement.
acceleration_effect.evidence: Compared to RL using outcome rewards only, PRIME achieves a 2.5x sample efficiency gain and a 6.9% performance improvement on challenging math problems.

online_prm_importance.value: Updating the PRM online is key to the success of PRIME.
online_prm_importance.evidence: Our analysis shows that updating the PRM online is key to the success of PRIME.

inference_scaling_effect.value: PRIME brings substantial performance gain (16.7%) over Eurus-2-7B-SFT.
inference_scaling_effect.evidence: Notably, PRIME brings substantial performance gain (16.7%) over Eurus-2-7B-SFT.

main_findings.value: PRIME greatly benefits sample efficiency and policy performance while being easy to use with minimum cost.
main_findings.evidence: Throughout the experiments, we validate that PRIME greatly benefits sample efficiency and policy performance.

technical_contributions.value: The introduction of implicit process rewards for scalable online learning of dense rewards in RL.
technical_contributions.evidence: We propose PRIME, a scalable online RL method with dense rewards.

practical_contributions.value: PRIME reduces the development overhead by eliminating the dedicated reward modeling stage.
practical_contributions.evidence: PRIME also eliminates the dedicated reward modeling stage, which is required by existing works.

community_contributions.value: The open-source implementation of PRIME for broader community use.
community_contributions.evidence: The code is available at https://github.com/PRIME-RL/PRIME.
